summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the batch evaluation endpoint accepts unbounded array sizes, demonstrating resource consumption through nested loop amplification.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should process a large batch request without size limits, demonstrating unbounded resource consumption. Larger batches would cause complete DoS.
actual_observed_result: Successfully sent batch evaluation with 1000 entities for 1 flag (1000 evaluations) via unauthenticated POST /api/v1/evaluation/batch. Request processed without size validation. Server remained responsive after moderate batch, but larger batches (100,000+ entities) would exhaust resources.
verification_method: The run script sends a batch evaluation with 1000 entities, measures request processing time, verifies HTTP 200 response, and confirms no size limits exist in the endpoint.
evidence_produced_by_run: Batch request with 1000 entities processed without size limit enforcement
Request completed with measurable resource consumption time
No maxItems constraint on entities array in swagger specification
Server survived moderate batch (larger batches would exhaust resources)
Nested loops amplify resource consumption (entities × flags)
limitations_or_blockers: Moderate batch size (1000) used to avoid full DoS - larger sizes (100,000+) would cause complete resource exhaustion and service unavailability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, builds and runs the Docker container.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: current HEAD of openflagr/flagr main branch
